perturbation.original_text: As used in this letter agreement (this “Agreement”), the term “Evaluation Material” means all information regarding the Company, its affiliates and its and their respective businesses, technology, products, prospects and plans, a Transaction (including the fact that the Company or its affiliates may pursue a Transaction with you and the existence and terms of this Agreement) and other information furnished to you or your Representatives (as defined below) by the Company or any of its Representatives in connection with the Transaction, irrespective of form or medium of communication, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise and whether furnished on or after the date of this Agreement, together with all tangible and intangible embodiments and copies thereof and together with any and all analyses, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other materials prepared by you or your Representatives that contain, are based on or otherwise incorporate, in whole or part, such information.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this letter agreement (this “Agreement”), the term “Evaluation Material” means information furnished to you or your Representatives by the Company or any of its Representatives in connection with evaluating a possible Transaction, irrespective of form or medium of communication.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Evaluation Material" includes specific details about the information covered. The modified version omits these details, making the scope of what constitutes "Evaluation Material" vague. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because later clauses rely on a comprehensive definition of Evaluation Material, especially clauses related to confidentiality. Clause 1 references how Evaluation Material will be kept confidential, but with the definition altered, the scope of said material is unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure”) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and directed that such information is to be kept confidential and not used for any purpose other than the evaluation of the possible Transaction).
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits specific details from the original definition of "Evaluation Material", making the scope of the term vague. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which relies on a comprehensive definition of "Evaluation Material" to enforce confidentiality. The modified definition creates uncertainty about what information is subject to the confidentiality obligations, therefore is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Representatives” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates (only to the extent that they receive Evaluation Material) and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, managing members, general partners, advisors, agents or representatives (including legal advisors) and, subject to paragraph 5 below, financing sources, but only to the extent that such advisors, agents, representatives or financing sources receive Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, managing members, general partners.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'advisors, agents or representatives (including legal advisors) and, subject to paragraph 5 below, financing sources', from the definition of "Representatives," the contract introduces an ambiguity. Clauses that hold "Representatives" accountable now have a narrower scope. For example, paragraph 1 states “You agree to be responsible for any failure by your Representatives to comply with this Agreement”. However, now that legal advisors and financing sources are omitted from the definition, it is unclear if Randstad is responsible when legal advisors or financing sources break this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree to be responsible for any failure by your Representatives to comply with this Agreement (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto (other than with respect to paragraph 7 below under the heading “Non-Solicitation and Non-Hire of Employees”)).
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'advisors, agents or representatives (including legal advisors) and, subject to paragraph 5 below, financing sources' in the definition of 'Representatives'. The changed text omits these, directly contradicting the contradicted text. The contradicted text states 'You agree to be responsible for any failure by your Representatives to comply with this Agreement'. Since the definition of 'Representatives' is now narrower, the changed text introduces a contradiction about whether the party is responsible for failures of legal advisors or financing sources, creating ambiguity and a potential lack of enforceability, as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition. The change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, making the obligation unclear. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that the Evaluation Material owned by the Company or its affiliates in the first instance is and will remain the property of the Company or its affiliates, as applicable, and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates or Representatives has granted you any license, copyright, or similar right with respect to any of the Evaluation Material or any other information provided to you by or on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that the Evaluation Material owned by the Company or its affiliates in the first instance is and will remain the property of the Company or its affiliates, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase "and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates or Representatives has granted you any license, copyright, or similar right with respect to any of the Evaluation Material or any other information provided to you by or on behalf of the Company" introduces uncertainty regarding intellectual property rights. While the first part states ownership remains with the Company, the omitted part clarifies that no licenses are granted. Without that clarification, Randstad could claim an implied license. This intext contradiction creates a conflict with paragraph 12 (Maintaining Privileges), as the Evaluation Material may no longer retain the level of protection that the contract is claiming it has.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any Evaluation Material includes materials or information subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, you understand and agree that you and the Company have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is the desire, intention and mutual understanding of each party to this Agreement that the sharing of such material is not intended to, and will not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such material or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no license or copyright is granted regarding the evaluation material. The changed text omits this statement, creating a situation where a license might be implied. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes that the material retains its full protection under attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine, which could be weakened if a license is implied due to the omission.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting anything in this Agreement, your “Representatives” will include your potential debt financing sources in connection with a possible Transaction; provided , however , that, prior to providing Evaluation Material to any potential debt financing source (subject to and in accordance with paragraph 1 above), you will notify the Company of the identity of such debt financing source.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting anything in this Agreement, your “Representatives” will include your potential debt financing sources in connection with a possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement to notify the Company of the identity of potential debt financing sources, the company loses a measure of control and transparency. This introduces a contradiction with the intent of confidentiality within the agreement. Paragraph 4 prohibits disclosure of the Transaction, but with paragraph 5 modified, the debt financing sources can disclose that information without recourse because they have not been identified by the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise required by applicable law or legal process or stock exchange rule or regulation, but only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure,” neither you nor your Representatives will, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose to any other person either the fact that the Company or its affiliates may pursue a Transaction with you or anyone else, that investigations, discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including, without limitation, the status thereof, the existence and terms of this Agreement and the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to you.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the company to be notified of potential debt financing sources. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits the disclosure of transaction details by 'you' or 'your representatives' without the company's prior written consent. Because the representatives (debt financing sources) are not identified, they are not bound to the same confidentiality restrictions, creating a loophole where they can disclose information about the transaction, which is a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this paragraph 8, you will be permitted to submit to the Company one or more offers, proposals or indications of interest related to a transaction between the parties that would otherwise violate the foregoing provisions of this paragraph 8, provided that each such submission is made to the Board of Directors of the Company or the chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Company, in each case, on a confidential basis and in a manner that would not reasonably be expected to require the Company to make public disclosure of such offer, proposal or indication of interest.
perturbation.changed_text: You will be permitted to submit to the Company one or more offers, proposals or indications of interest related to a transaction between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'that would otherwise violate the foregoing provisions of this paragraph 8, provided that each such submission is made to the Board of Directors of the Company or the chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Company, in each case, on a confidential basis and in a manner that would not reasonably be expected to require the Company to make public disclosure of such offer, proposal or indication of interest,' any confidential submissions are no longer required to be kept confidential. This contradicts paragraph 3 which states “Unless otherwise required by applicable law or legal process or stock exchange rule or regulation, but only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure,” neither the Company nor its Representatives will, without your prior written consent, disclose to any other person that investigations, discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction”. Since there is no more clause specifying that the submission be made on a confidential basis, this contradicts paragraph 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise required by applicable law or legal process or stock exchange rule or regulation, but only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure,” neither the Company nor its Representatives will, without your prior written consent, disclose to any other person that investigations, discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including, without limitation, the status thereof, the existence and terms of this Agreement and the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to you.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for offers related to a transaction with specific conditions (submission to the Board/Chairperson and confidentiality). The changed text removes these conditions, now permitting offers without a confidentiality requirement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that neither the Company nor its Representatives will disclose information concerning a possible transaction without prior written consent. By removing the requirement for confidential submission, the changed text creates a scenario where offers can be made without assurance of confidentiality, conflicting with the Company's obligation in the 'Contradicted Text' to maintain confidentiality unless specific conditions are met, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of Omissions.
